But the transcript shows TWO 'n' commands. This means the player typed 'n', got the response, then typed 'n' again. But at 1fps, both 'n' typings appear in the same frame (frame 13) as historical commands.

So my typing-tracking approach misses the second 'n' because it only captures changes in the typing line. To capture all commands, I need to also look at the TRANSCRIPT (historical commands) which shows ALL previously executed commands.

I need a hybrid approach: use the typing-tracking for the sequence, but also ensure we capture commands that appear in the transcript that weren't captured by typing-tracking.
